topic: heavy storm damage to pond 2 netting

turns.content: friend big storm last night. very bad. i wake up 4am, go outside, everything mess. pond 2 netting all tear up
That sounds rough — a major storm can do a lot of damage to pond netting quickly. How bad is it? Is the netting completely destroyed or just torn in some sections?
maybe 40% tear. wind pull stake out from ground, you know. netting just flapping around in the water. i tie some back this morning but it not hold good

40% torn is significant but salvageable. The stakes pulling out is actually the bigger concern — if the anchoring system fails, even repaired netting won't survive the next storm. What kind of stakes were you using — wood or metal?
wood stake. bamboo some of them. ground very soft after rain, you know. stake just slide out. and already 3 heron come this morning. they smart bird. they know when net is down

Herons are incredibly quick to spot an opening — they'll be back every day until the netting is fixed. For right now, string some fishing line low across the pond surface. Birds can feel it with their legs and wings and won't want to land. Works much better than scarecrows.

For the stakes — bamboo and wood in wet delta soil is always going to be a problem. Have you considered rebar stakes instead? 12mm rebar driven in at a 45 degree angle, about 60-70cm deep. Much harder for wind to pull out.
rebar. that is metal bar right? i see some farmer use it. cost more than bamboo though

Yes, rebar is steel reinforcing bar. It does cost more upfront, but bamboo stakes in wet ground basically need replacing every storm. Over a year or two, rebar is actually cheaper because you set it once and forget it.

Here's a good system: space the rebar stakes about 1.5 to 2 meters apart, run a heavy tension rope along the perimeter connecting all the stakes, then hang the netting from the rope. That way the wind force distributes across the whole line instead of pulling on individual stakes.
okay that make sense. rope take the force, not the stake direct. i like that idea, you know. simple. how old is your netting on pond 2 — should i patch or replace whole thing?

That's a question for you — how old is the netting? If it's more than 2-3 years old and made of regular PE material, patching is usually a waste of time. Sun makes it brittle and it'll just tear somewhere else next storm.
maybe 3 year already. PE material yes. already brittle before storm. sun damage, you know. i think replace whole thing better. what material last longer in this climate?

For replacement, go with HDPE netting — high-density polyethylene, UV stabilized. It costs about 20-30% more than regular PE but lasts 4-5 years even in tropical sun. Look for "UV stabilized" on the label. Mesh size around 15-20mm is standard for predator netting.

When you buy, get about 15% extra length for overlap at the edges. Tight overlap at the ground line is where most birds try to sneak in.

okay good. HDPE, UV stabilized. i write that down. my worker can help install tomorrow morning. today i put fishing line like you say, keep heron away tonight. one more night with no net is okay i think

One more night with the fishing line trick should be fine. The herons mostly hunt at dawn and dusk, so get the line up before sunset. Then tomorrow you can do the full install — rebar stakes, tension rope, new HDPE netting. It'll be a much stronger system than what you had before. Good luck with the repair!
thank you friend. storm is part of this life, you know. every year something break. but we fix and keep going. okay i go buy rebar and netting now before shop close
That's the right attitude — storms come and go but the ponds stay. Get the materials today, fishing line up tonight, and you'll have a better setup than before by tomorrow. Take care!
